name: Attribute is not duplicated
description: This rule checks that HTML and SVG starting tags do not contain duplicated attributes.
applicability_text:
This rule applies to any [starting tag](https://www.w3.org/TR/html5/syntax.html#start-tags) in an HTML or SVG document.

**Note:** This rule cannot be tested on the DOM Tree because the browser removes duplicates of any attribute that is already present on an element.
expectations: For each test target, there are no duplicated [attributes](https://www.w3.org/TR/html5/syntax.html#elements-attributes).

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: This `img` element contains no duplicated attributes.

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: This `br` element contains no attributes, so there is no duplicated attribute.

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: This `input` element contains three different attributes, two of them being empty.

examples.name: Passed Example 5
examples.description: This `script` element contains duplicated attributes, but they are ignored because they are placed within the `script` tag.

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: This `img` element contains a duplicated `alt` attribute.

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: This `input` element contains a duplicate `disabled` attribute.

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: This `line` element contains duplicate `x1` and duplicate `y1` attributes.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: This code is XML, not HTML or SVG.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: This code is JavaScript, not HTML or SVG.

accessibility_support: There are no accessibility support issues known.
